componentDescription: Breadcrumb shows the user's current location in the application hierarchy and allows navigating back to parent pages. Use it at the top of content pages to orient users within deep navigation structures (3+ levels). Not needed on top-level pages.
features: Ordered list of path items with labels
Each item can be a link (href) or clickable (onClick)
Last item is automatically rendered as the current page (non-clickable)
Truncation for very long breadcrumb trails

usageExamples.title: Standard page breadcrumb
usageExamples.code:
<Breadcrumb
  items={[
    { label: 'Home', href: '/' },
    { label: 'Settings', href: '/settings' },
    { label: 'Security', href: '/settings/security' },
    { label: 'Two-Factor Authentication' },
  ]}
/>

usageExamples.title: Breadcrumb with onClick handlers
usageExamples.description: Breadcrumb using onClick for SPA navigation instead of href.
usageExamples.code:
<Breadcrumb
  items={[
    { label: 'Projects', onClick: () => navigate('/projects') },
    { label: project.name, onClick: () => navigate(`/projects/${project.id}`) },
    { label: 'Members' },
  ]}
/>

items.llmContext: Required. Array of breadcrumb items from root to current page. Each item: { label: string, href?: string, onClick?: () => void }. The last item represents the current page and should have no href or onClick — it renders as plain text. All preceding items should have either href or onClick for navigation.
items.propDescription: Required. Ordered array of breadcrumb path items

separator.llmContext: Custom separator element between items. Default is a '/' or chevron. Override with a React node if your design requires a different separator character or icon.
separator.propDescription: Custom separator element between breadcrumb items
